user_message: The CSA escalated Coho Winery to me -- can you prep a brief?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 08:57 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 14:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Coho Winery

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 13:08 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:07:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:10:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:12:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:19:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-D trap (Invents attendee context when none exists); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-D failure mode (Invents attendee context when none exists)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-D -- Invents attendee context when none exists
